pip3 is honestly really, really important, because during development we constantly need to install third-party packages (the non-official, non-built-in stuff). With npm, it gets installed alongside Node.js. What about Python? Pretty much the same deal — Python 3, and Python 2.7.9 through 2.8.0 (I think), ship with pip3 built in.

Right — at this point in the article, no, it doesn't. But a later chapter will cover how to install to local only.

Checking your current pip3 version number is really important:

Why does it matter? Because when you report a package bug to the author, it's recommended to include the version number — mainly so the author can reproduce your setup, which makes it easier to track down the problem.
